meta.name: Levente's AI Stack
meta.description: A full-featured Claude Code setup with superpowers, Figma, Atlassian, Slack, GitHub, and Chrome DevTools — built for product and engineering work.
meta.author: lhernadi

apps.description: Claude Code — the AI coding assistant this stack is built around
Node.js — required to run MCP servers via npx
Docker Desktop — required to run SearXNG for private local web search

plugins.description: Superpowers — skills, agents, brainstorming, TDD, planning, deep research (/deep-research), and more. The backbone of this stack.
GitHub — create PRs, review issues, search repos, and manage code directly from Claude
Atlassian — search Jira and Confluence, create issues, write specs, and run standup reports
Slack — summarize channels, find discussions, draft announcements, and generate standups
Figma — read designs, implement UI from Figma files, and manage code connect mappings
Chrome DevTools — debug web apps, audit performance, and automate browser interactions from Claude

mcp_servers.name: github
mcp_servers.description: GitHub MCP server — gives Claude direct access to your repos, PRs, and issues

mcp_servers.name: confluence
mcp_servers.description: Confluence MCP server — lets Claude read and write your company wiki pages

mcp_servers.name: jira
mcp_servers.description: Jira MCP server — lets Claude create, search, and update Jira issues

mcp_servers.name: figma
mcp_servers.description: Figma MCP server — gives Claude access to your Figma files and design tokens

mcp_servers.name: chrome-devtools
mcp_servers.description: Chrome DevTools MCP server — connects Claude to a running Chrome instance for debugging

credentials.label: Anthropic API Key
credentials.help: Create an account at console.anthropic.com, go to API Keys, and generate a new key

credentials.label: GitHub Personal Access Token
credentials.help: Go to GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Generate new token. Needs repo, read:org scopes.

credentials.label: Atlassian API Token (for Confluence)
credentials.help: Go to id.atlassian.com → Security → Create and manage API tokens

credentials.label: Your Atlassian Email
credentials.help: The email address you use to log in to Confluence/Jira

credentials.label: Confluence Base URL
credentials.help: Your Confluence wiki URL, e.g. https://yourcompany.atlassian.net/wiki

credentials.label: Atlassian API Token (for Jira)
credentials.help: Same token as Confluence — go to id.atlassian.com → Security → API tokens

credentials.label: Your Jira Email
credentials.help: The email address you use to log in to Jira

credentials.label: Jira Base URL
credentials.help: Your Jira instance URL, e.g. https://yourcompany.atlassian.net

credentials.label: Figma Personal Access Token
credentials.help: In Figma, go to Account Settings → Personal access tokens → Generate new token

scripts.description: Install the SearXNG web search hook script into ~/.claude/scripts/

scripts.run: docker pull searx/searx && docker run -d --name searxng -p 8080:8080 --restart unless-stopped searx/searx
scripts.description: Pull and start SearXNG — a self-hosted, privacy-respecting search engine used by the web search hook
Install the deep-research skill — an enterprise-grade 8-phase research pipeline with source credibility scoring. Use /deep-research in Claude Code.
Install Council of High Intelligence — 18 AI personas (Aristotle, Feynman, Kahneman, Torvalds & more) deliberate your hardest decisions. Use /council in Claude Code.

tour.title: Welcome to your AI stack!
tour.content:
You've just installed a full-featured Claude Code setup. Here's what's now available:

- **Superpowers** — type /brainstorm, /commit, /deep-research, or /council to unlock advanced workflows
- **Web search** — Claude searches via your local SearXNG instance (private, no tracking)
- **GitHub** — ask Claude to review PRs, create issues, or search your repos
- **Atlassian** — ask Claude to search Jira, write Confluence pages, or triage bugs
- **Slack** — ask Claude to summarize channels or draft announcements
- **Figma** — paste a Figma URL and ask Claude to implement the design
- **Chrome DevTools** — ask Claude to debug your running web app

tour.title: Try this first
tour.content:
Open a project folder and try one of these:

```
/brainstorm  → plan a new feature with AI assistance
/commit      → generate a git commit message from your changes
/deep-research <topic> → multi-source research with a written report
```

Or ask Claude anything about your codebase — it can read files, run tests, and make changes.
